recent_calls.summary: The user called to request a follow-up appointment and asked to speak with a live agent. The initial agent, Sophie, transferred the call to another agent to assist the user further.

John Fletcher called to schedule a service appointment for his air conditioning system, which is not responding to the thermostat and won't turn on. The agent confirmed the appointment for Monday, April 6th at 11 a.m. and collected the home warranty dispatch number. John provided feedback rating the AI assistant a 4 and suggested informing callers that they would be speaking with an AI before the call.

Alex from American Home Shield called to schedule a home warranty dispatch for a central air conditioning unit that is not cooling. The agent confirmed the details, corrected the customer's name and address, and noted the preferred service date as early as April 3rd. The call ended with the user declining maintenance program information and providing positive feedback on the service.
The call was very brief with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The user requested to speak with someone, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

Jett Kennedy called to request an update on the HVAC replacement quote for both heating and cooling systems. The agent confirmed details and noted that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing will follow up as soon as possible. Jett declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

Frank D'Antonio called to transfer a claim from American Home Shield for a down draft issue with a Wolf exhaust fan. The agent collected all necessary details and scheduled a service appointment with a preference to speak with a technician before the visit. Frank declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The user requested to speak with a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to follow up on a missed callback regarding their AC tune-up. The agent apologized for the delay and transferred the call to someone who could provide live updates. The call ended with a successful transfer to another representative.

The user, Cort, called on behalf of Morgan Bennett to schedule a service appointment for a cooling unit that is blowing warm air and not cooling. The agent collected necessary details including service address, preferred appointment time, and warranty information, and informed the user that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would follow up. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided neutral feedback, expressing a preference for speaking with a real person.

Deborah called to confirm a service appointment for her heating system making noise, possibly related to the thermostat. The agent booked her a provisional appointment for April 3rd at 10:00 AM and confirmed her contact details. Deborah provided positive feedback about the agent's personality and understanding.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to another agent. The initial agent facilitated the transfer promptly without any issues.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another representative. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to inquire about heating and cooling services and mentioned a billing issue with a previous service. The agent acknowledged the issue and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance.

Delores called to schedule a drain line installation replacement and was provisionally booked for April 14th in the morning. The agent confirmed the details and the user was satisfied with the service, providing positive feedback. The call ended successfully with no issues.

The user inquired about the Budget Club maintenance program for twice-yearly furnace and air conditioner cleaning. They scheduled a spring cleaning service for the outside HVAC unit on Monday morning, April 6th, and the agent provisionally booked the appointment, informing the user about the dispatch fee and follow-up confirmation. The user declined to provide feedback and ended the call.

Arthur Word called to confirm details about his semiannual HVAC checkup scheduled for 12 o'clock tomorrow and requested a call about pricing and club membership benefits before the visit. The agent confirmed the appointment details and passed the request to the team. The booking attempt failed due to an existing appointment or availability issue.

Sandra called to set up a new appointment for an air conditioner checkup through American Home Shield at her home in Overland. The agent confirmed details including the dispatch number and scheduled an afternoon appointment. Sandra was satisfied with the service and provided positive feedback.

Arthur called to schedule a semiannual HVAC checkup and inspection of a recently installed whole house humidifier. The agent booked an appointment for Thursday at 12:00 p.m. and informed Arthur that the team would confirm the exact time and discuss the dispatch fee. Arthur expressed some concern about not knowing the cost but was satisfied with securing the appointment.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, greeted the user and then initiated a call transfer to another agent.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to inquire about a technician who was expected before noon but had not arrived yet. The agent apologized for the delay and transferred the call to someone who could provide live updates. The call ended with a successful transfer to another representative.

The user called to check the status of a home insurance warranty service request for seeding, which had already been assessed and approved. The agent confirmed the details and informed the user that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact them shortly. The call ended with the user being transferred to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested customer service and the agent transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was successful and the call ended shortly after.
The user called back after missing a call from the agent. The agent attempted to connect the user with a live agent, but the call ended abruptly shortly after.

The user called to get an update on a home warranty authorization and requested to speak with a live agent. The initial agent attempted to assist but then transferred the call to a live agent as requested. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.

Angela from American Home Shield called Budget to provide dispatch details and discuss a service issue with a combo heat and AC unit. She explained that a heat exchanger replacement was authorized but the status was not updated, and the member was still experiencing issues. The call ended with the agent transferring Angela to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Jesse from American Onship, called to get a status update on a work order related to heating and cooling. The AI agent collected the dispatch number and attempted to connect the user to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer to a live agent.
The user requested to speak to a representative and the agent transferred the call to a live agent successfully. The call lasted 14 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

The user, Ingrid Johnson, called to inquire if a technician would come today. The agent informed her that the appointment is scheduled for April 8th and then transferred the call to a live agent as requested by the user.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

David Farley called to inquire about the appointment time for service at 2811 Harding Street. The agent confirmed the appointment for April 2nd between 8:00 and 9:00 a.m. and assisted David in scheduling a separate visit for 2113 Ohio. The call ended with the agent confirming a follow-up for the new appointment request.
The user, Mikaela, returned a call to speak with Dan but was transferred to another agent for assistance. The call was quickly transferred to a live agent to continue helping the user.
The user requested to speak to a live representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to reschedule a missed final inspection for a newly installed central air and furnace system. The agent confirmed the details and offered available time slots, but the booking attempt failed due to a scheduling conflict. The agent assured the user that a human agent would follow up to finalize the appointment.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was promptly transferred to another agent for further assistance.

Elvin called Budget to schedule a service appointment related to a home warranty for HVAC issues. The agent confirmed the service type and ownership status but the call ended before the appointment was scheduled. The conversation was brief and focused on gathering initial information.

The user called to schedule a home warranty service appointment through American Home Shield for heating and cooling system inspection. The agent clarified the nature of the request and asked for a preferred appointment time, but the user decided to check their schedule and call back later to provide the details. The call ended with the user planning to call back to finalize the appointment.

Frank McCoy called to schedule a routine AC inspection under his American Home Shield home warranty. The agent confirmed the details, including the dispatch number and preferred appointment time, and noted Frank's interest in receiving information about home maintenance programs. The call concluded with the agent assuring a follow-up from the team and Frank declining to provide feedback due to his schedule.

The user called to address an issue with not receiving an e-verification email for signature verification. The agent confirmed the user's email address and last name spelling, updated the email address, and assured the user that the e-verification email would be resent. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further assistance needed.

The user called to inquire about the time frame for an appointment under the name Emily Sadler. The agent could not find the appointment and offered to connect the user to a live representative, which the user requested. The call was then successfully transferred to a live agent.

Frank McCoy called to schedule a routine air conditioner checkup under his American Home Shield home warranty. He provided his dispatch number and preferred a Friday after 9:00 a.m. appointment. The agent confirmed the details and noted Frank's tentative interest in maintenance programs, with a follow-up expected from the team.

Joe called back to reschedule a missed AC cleaning service. He requested a standard AC cleaning as a renter at 912 Saravalle Drive, Saint Peters, with a preferred time of anytime Sunday, but the agent informed him there were no available slots on Sunday and asked for alternative times. The phone number was corrected during the call.

The user called to set up an appointment for their air conditioner under their HSA home warranty service plan. The agent explained that the appointment could not be booked directly and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.
The user requested to speak to a real person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to clarify a service request that was changed by American Home Shield and mentioned being transferred regarding an HVAC tune-up. The agent attempted to understand the user's current service needs but the call ended abruptly before the user could provide more details.
The user called and requested to speak with Cece. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to the appropriate person.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another agent successfully. The call lasted 13 seconds and ended with a call transfer.
